title: flagged-issue.v1
description: A flagged transcript/attribution bug — the record that turns a live meeting defect into a reproducible OFFLINE test (O-TEL-3). A user OR the system (analyze.mjs auto-flag) raises one; it carries enough to locate the offending segment, links to the captured-signal.v1 / tape the signal lives in, and routes to the O-TEL-2 replay. issue_type derives from what analyze.mjs already detects (mis-attribution, oversegmentation).

IssueType.description: What kind of defect — derived from analyze.mjs's oracles (mis-attribution, oversegmentation) plus the loss/hallucination families.

FlaggedBy.description: Who raised it — a human via the feedback UI, the feedback API, or the system auto-flagger.

SignalLink.description: Where the offending raw signal lives, so the issue routes to the O-TEL-2 replay. At least one of captured_signal / tape must be present for the issue to be replayable.

captured_signal.description: path/uri to the captured-signal.v1 JSONL the segment's frames are in

tape.description: path/uri to the legacy raw-signal tape, when that is the stored form

frame_seq_start.description: first captured-signal frame seq covering the segment

frame_seq_end.description: last captured-signal frame seq covering the segment

FlaggedIssue.description: One flagged issue. The locator (platform/native/session/segment/speaker/text/start/end) identifies the offending segment; signal links it to the replayable raw signal.

issue_id.description: stable id for the flagged issue

trace_id.description: the distributed trace_id (logevent.v1) of the meeting/segment where the defect occurred — pulls every structured log across the system for that meeting AND links to the captured-signal.v1 with the same trace_id, so the bug routes to both its full cross-system trace and its deterministic O-TEL-2 replay

speaker.description: the label the segment carried (the disputed attribution)

ground_truth.description: the correct value (e.g. the true speaker / the correct text), when known
